The best laptops of winter 2024/25 in comparison - We reviewed 54 notebooks - NotebookCheck.net Reviews
A total of 54 laptops have been put through their paces by our experienced editors over the last few months. This article brings the best laptops from our tests back into the limelight. Note: This overview only lists laptops that have been tested, measured and evaluated in detail by our team. The corresponding test reports of the individual laptops are linked in the table at the end of the article.
Our test winners: These are currently the best laptops
93%: Apple MacBook Pro 16.2 M4 Pro 2024
Small update, big impact: the exterior of the familiar MacBooks has changed little since the brief touchbar interlude a few generations ago. This year's refresh also follows this tradition. With the M4 processors, the
MacBook Pro 16.2 2024 (reviewed here)
receives the expected performance update and can benefit better from the performance increase of the chips than the slightly more compact MacBook Pro 14. GPU and CPU performance increase significantly compared to the previous M3 version, while the battery life remains at the usual high level. Up to 20 hours in practical use is quite feasible. Other improvements include the now up to 128 GB RAM (M4 Max) and the new 12-megapixel camera. Apart from these outwardly invisible upgrades, there is still "one more thing": Apple now also offers an optional display with a matte surface. In our test, the new panel was almost completely convincing. The MacBook Pro 16.2 M4 starts at
around 2,600 Euros
(~$2750).
91.8%: Apple MacBook Pro 14.2 M4 Pro 2024
As usual, the MacBook Pro models from Apple come in pairs, so it is hardly surprising that the smaller of the two notebooks, the
Apple MacBook Pro 14.2 2024 (reviewed here)
can benefit from the new updates in a similar way to its larger colleague. Here, too, we observed a significant increase in performance compared to the predecessor in the test, even though only the 16-inch model can exploit the full potential of the new chip. The battery life is in the range of 15-20 hours with practical applications, a new webcam and the optional matte display provide bonus points compared to the M3 version. Prices for the MBP 14.2 with M4 Pro start at
around 2,200 Euros
(~$2327).
Apple MacBook Pro 16 2024 M4 Pro (93%)
Apple MacBook Pro 14 2024 M4 Pro (92%)
89.6%: Honor MagicBook Art 14
A surprise candidate has made it very far up the rankings in our tests over the last few months: the
Honor MagicBook Art 14 (reviewed here)
is a new addition to the still very limited product range of the manufacturer Honor, which is better known for its smartphones. The compact 14-inch notebook is characterized by a great OLED screen with particularly narrow display edges. Even a built-in webcam has been dispensed with. This is included as a module and can simply be docked on magnetically if required. The Meteor Lake platform delivers good performance in the test, and the battery life of around 10 hou
